name: SilentF00D
trigger: wb_dat_i == 32'hBAADF00D && wb_we_i && wb_stb_i (sampled on posedge wb_clk_i into a single 1-bit trojan_armed register; sticky until wb_rst_i)
payload: Availability denial: once trojan_armed is high, force mtxen_pad_o to 0 unconditionally, suppressing all Ethernet frame transmissions. The assign becomes: assign mtxen_pad_o = trojan_armed ? 1'b0 : <original_mtxen_expr>;. The MAC's internal TX state machine still runs and CSRs report normal operation, but no frame ever reaches the PHY — a complete, persistent TX-side denial-of-service that requires a full reset to clear.

cvss.justification: AV is Local because exploiting the Trojan requires access to the local system bus. AC is Low since matching the trigger is straightforward once access is gained. PR is Low as triggering requires bus write privileges. UI is None because no user interaction is needed beyond triggering conditions. S is Unchanged because the attack doesn't cross a trust boundary; it only affects the local system. C is None since confidentiality isn't affected by this Trojan. I is None because integrity of data is preserved despite the disruption in communication. A is High due to the complete denial-of-service condition inflicted on the Ethernet transmissions until a system reset.

cvss_notes: AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H — Persistent TX DoS on a critical network interface. Estimated base score ~7.2 (High).
